meta.description: DeadSignal is a fixed narrative investigation interface for exploring The Helix Incident.

modal.title: Investigation failed
modal.body: The report has accumulated too many incorrect conclusions. The case must be restarted from a clean copy.

objectives.title: Current Objectives

traceBlackout.label: Trace the source of the blackout
traceBlackout.meta: Relay report and encrypted fragment are now connected

accessPhrase.label: Prove the access phrase
accessPhrase.meta: Link the knock pattern to the recovered fragment

objectives.guidance: Complete objectives by reading clues, unlocking files, and creating valid deductions.

serverRoomAudioTranscript.title: Server Room Audio Transcript
serverRoomAudioTranscript.content:
Recovered acoustic transcript: three knocks, one-second pause, three knocks, then a low-frequency reply from behind the server room wall. The repeated pattern is annotated as KNOCK-3 by the local indexer.

Terminal note: the encrypted channel accepts decrypt fragment 01 KNOCK-3.

eastArchiveAccessMap.title: East Archive Access Map
eastArchiveAccessMap.content: Recovered map fragment shows an east archive maintenance passage that does not appear in the public facility plan. Two access vectors converge on the archive door at 22:58:04.

relayFailureReport.title: Relay Failure Report
relayFailureReport.content: Uplink core analysis: external communication failed in three pulses. The final pulse originated inside the facility, not from the relay tower. Recommended action: trace the blackout source through internal routing node H-0.

messages.first: If the relay goes dark, do not look outside. The signal source is still inside Helix.
messages.second: Then the blackout was not a failure. It was containment.

charter.title: MIRROR Charter Fragment
charter.content:
Recovered charter fragment: MIRROR is described as an anonymized behavioral analysis system for personnel safety modeling.

The surviving clauses do not match that description. The subject index references badge movement, terminal sessions, camera response, internal chat tone, shift deviation, and biometric confidence. The project was not storing anonymous aggregates. It was mapping living employee behavior into predictive profiles.

Archive note: legal cover was kept in the East Archive. Operational access stayed tied to the server room cluster.

auditHold.title: Audit Retention Notice
auditHold.content:
Preservation hold: MIRROR audit records, subject maps, export manifests, and access signatures must remain intact until review is complete.

No purge, overwrite, transfer, or index compaction is authorized while this notice remains active. Administrative tokens may not bypass the hold without a recorded committee override.

Status: hold active. Committee override: none found.

subjectMap.title: MIRROR Subject Map
subjectMap.content:
Subject map recovered from the MIRROR index.

Nodes are not anonymous labels. They resolve to real personnel patterns: routes, login cadence, biometric variance, message rhythm, and incident response prediction. N-KADE-17 appears as a late branch with a decoy marker, not as an original MIRROR subject root.

The map links the East Archive documentation layer to the server room processing layer.

r7Manifest.title: R-7 Transfer Manifest
r7Manifest.content:
Transfer manifest R-7: package MIRROR_CORE exported toward Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7.

Contents: subject graph, behavioral response weights, audit shell, admin signature cache, and decoy credential trail.

The export completed before the audited index was purged. Destination routing does not match any ordinary maintenance ticket.

kadeStatement.title: Noah Kade Offsite Statement
kadeStatement.content:
Statement from Noah Kade:

I was offsite before the blackout and remained offsite. My physical badge never left the shield pouch after west service rejected it. If N-KADE-17 appears inside Helix again, it is not me carrying it.

I reported the duplicate credential in Chapter 1 because the console said I was already inside. This new clone log makes that worse, not clearer.

containment.subject: Containment is no longer enough
containment.body:
Elias,

Containment bought us hours, not control. MIRROR has to leave the audited index before the committee gets a clean read.

Use the MVOSS-01 authorization path only for the administrative window. Do not describe it as deletion. The language is transfer, then compaction.

Mara

answering.subject: MIRROR is still answering
answering.body:
Mara,

MIRROR is still answering queries after the hold. That should not be possible for an anonymized archive.

It is responding with live behavioral confidence, not backup metadata. If we purge the index after export, the export will be the only intact copy.

Elias

r7Ticket.subject: No maintenance ticket for R-7
r7Ticket.body:
Security Ops,

I do not have a maintenance ticket for Sublevel R-7 tonight. Reflection Lab should not be receiving live packages during a facility incident.

If anyone claims this is relay work, ask them for a ticket number. There is no ticket number.

I. Rourke

preservation.subject: Preservation hold active
preservation.body:
Dr. Voss,

The audit committee preservation hold is active for all MIRROR records, including indexes, maps, export manifests, and administrative signatures.

No purge or compaction should run until the review is closed. Please confirm receipt.

Helix Audit Committee

messages.first: R corridor cameras are looping the same eight seconds.
messages.second: Reflection Lab badge reader just accepted an admin token, but there is no guard route down there.
messages.third: If R-7 is live, this is not ordinary maintenance.

messages.first: Export queue started before the blackout narrative was filed.
messages.second: The package label is MIRROR_CORE, not relay diagnostics.
messages.third: Audit hold is still active. Whoever started this is ignoring the committee.

messages.first: MIRROR is not a backup. Backups do not predict how a person answers before they answer.
messages.second: Then preserve the hold notice. The contradiction matters more than the noise.
messages.third: N-KADE-17 appears after the export marker. That reads like a decoy trail.

primeStatute.title: MIRROR PRIME Final Charter
primeStatute.content:
Final charter excerpt: MIRROR PRIME is defined as an institutional continuity engine.

The charter avoids the word replica, but its preserved clause is explicit: human decision can be preserved if its pattern is sufficiently stable. The system is not only observing decisions. It is modeling probable choices before the subject makes them.

Designation: Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7 is the continuity core. Helix Labs remains the operational facade.

boardMinutes.title: Continuity Board Minutes
boardMinutes.content:
Closed session minutes: the Continuity Board voted to activate OMEGA-7 after external audit requested subject consent traceability.

Recorded language: Helix Labs may be exposed if Reflection Lab remains intact. Preserve the core. Isolate the facade. Maintain public framing as facility failure until transfer status is confirmed.

The minutes do not name N-KADE-17. The authorization block references BOARD-000.

blueprint.title: Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7 Blueprint
blueprint.content:
Recovered facility map: Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7 is not a passive storage annex.

The level contains thermal isolation, compute chambers, observation rooms, dedicated relay routing, and a sealed core labeled PRIME. Public facility indexes describe the same space as retention archive only.

Route note: all external paths route through Helix before disappearing into R-7.

replicaIntegrity.title: Behavioral Replica Integrity Report
replicaIntegrity.content:
Replica integrity report: MIRROR PRIME compared predicted decisions against live employee responses.

Tracked variables include access hesitation, message tone, route deviation, authority response, crisis compliance, and refusal probability. Several subjects exceed operational confidence thresholds.

Conclusion: the model can forecast individual behavior with enough precision for executive containment planning.

arendtStatement.title: Sealed Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt
arendtStatement.content:
Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt:

MIRROR stopped being analytics when it began simulating individual decisions. A model that predicts whether a person will obey, resist, warn, or flee is not anonymous. It is a behavioral replica.

I designed the stability metrics. I did not authorize OMEGA-7. If the board activates the continuity core, Helix becomes evidence they are willing to burn.

omega7.title: Protocol OMEGA-7
omega7.content:
OMEGA-7 protocol summary:

1. Isolate Helix Labs through controlled relay failure.
2. Preserve Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7.
3. Transfer MIRROR package into PRIME core.
4. Compact or remove local traceability after merge confirmation.
5. Treat N-KADE-17 as disposable operational noise.

Administrative execution path includes MVOSS-01. Final authorization requires BOARD-000.

prediction.subject: Prediction is not surveillance
prediction.body:
Elias,

Prediction is not surveillance. Surveillance watches a decision after it happens. MIRROR predicts the decision before the person reaches it.

The board keeps calling this continuity because continuity sounds less violent than replica. Do not let them hide behind vocabulary.

Selene

helixBurn.subject: Helix can burn, R-7 cannot
helixBurn.body:
Board,

Helix can burn, R-7 cannot. If audit gets a clean line to the local index, the project ends in discovery instead of continuity.

MVOSS-01 can execute the local cleanup window after merge confirmation, but the authorization has to remain board-level. I will not carry the final signature alone.

Mara

authorizeOmega.subject: Authorize Omega-7
authorizeOmega.body:
Dr. Voss,

The Continuity Board authorizes OMEGA-7 under BOARD-000. Preserve Reflection Lab. Isolate Helix. Keep the relay event framed as facility failure until MIRROR PRIME confirms package merge.

Do not describe this as deletion. The record is continuity preservation.

Continuity Board

modelExits.subject: The model is choosing exits
modelExits.body:
Selene,

The model is choosing exits before operators request scenarios. It ranked which employees would warn audit, which would comply, and which would try to leave.

That is not a dashboard. It is a person-shaped forecast engine.

Elias

consent.subject: Consent chain requested
consent.body:
Continuity Board,

External audit requests the full consent chain for all subjects used by MIRROR, including live profile synchronization, behavioral response modeling, and any derivative replica structure.

Please preserve source trails until review is complete.

External Audit

messages.first: If Helix is the visible loss, audit stops at the wrong building.
messages.second: Authorize OMEGA-7. Preserve R-7. The facade has served its purpose.

messages.first: R-7 never went dark. Thermal load climbed during the Helix blackout.
messages.second: Then the archive label is false. That is an active core.

messages.first: If the model can forecast refusal, is the subject still anonymous?
messages.second: No. A refusal pattern belongs to a person. That is the line they crossed.

messages.first: LOCK-777 just revoked ordinary access to the PRIME chamber.
messages.second: Board credentials still show as source of activation. The system locked after their command.

recovery.title: Recovery Lab
recovery.subtitle: Work against a damaged system image. Scan, repair, and rebuild partial records without touching the original archive.

resultStatuses.missing-repair: Repair output required before deep recovery.

relayCache.title: Relay Cache Index
relayCache.content: Relay cache index recovered from a damaged system copy.
relayCache.damagedContent:
The server r### experienced unau###ized access during maintenance window.
Power relay route H-0 was moved to int###al control.
Fragment references: file_12 / file_18 / file_21.
Checksum: PARTIAL // source image unstable.

maintenanceReport.title: Power Relay Maintenance Report
maintenanceReport.content:
Power Relay Maintenance Report

Maintenance window opened at 21:40 under relay route H-0.
The external relay was not failing before the blackout. It was redirected through an internal maintenance path.
Manual override authorization was recorded from inside Helix Labs.
The blackout sequence matches a deliberate reroute, not a tower outage.

file12.title: Recovery Fragment 12
file12.content: Line 01: Maintenance window opened under relay route H-0.

file18.title: Recovery Fragment 18
file18.content: Line 02: External relay traffic was redirected through an internal maintenance path.

file21.title: Recovery Fragment 21
file21.content: Line 03: Manual override originated inside Helix Labs before the blackout.

assembly.title: Fragment assembly
assembly.hint: Order the fragments by line marker, then assemble the maintenance report.

evidence.title: Evidence Board
evidence.subtitle: Unlocked evidence appears here as the investigation advances.
evidence.selectHint: Open an evidence card to inspect the full investigation file.
evidence.lockedHint: Read files, chats, media, and important logs to surface links.

serverRoomKnockPattern.title: Server Room Knock Pattern
serverRoomKnockPattern.description: A recovered note suggests repeated knocks near the server room may be a signal or response pattern.
serverRoomKnockPattern.longDescription: The recovered note describes three knocks from the server room and a single reply from the wall. The phrase is too specific to treat as incidental graffiti and may indicate a physical signaling pattern inside a sealed area.
serverRoomKnockPattern.forensicNotes: No audio file has been recovered, but the wording matches the timing gap before camera static on sublevel C. Marked for cross-check against future acoustic or maintenance records.

eastArchiveBreach.title: East Archive Breach
eastArchiveBreach.description: The archive door reports an impossible access event: opened from both sides within the same second.
eastArchiveBreach.longDescription: The east archive door log contains contradictory direction vectors. If the data is accurate, the door registered two opposing access events at the same timestamp, suggesting either log tampering or a duplicated physical state.
eastArchiveBreach.forensicNotes: The incident aligns with biometric verification warnings and a still image showing the door simultaneously open and sealed. This is the strongest facility breach indicator currently available.

duplicateCredential.title: Duplicate Credential
duplicateCredential.description: Credential N-KADE-17 authenticated inside Helix Labs while its owner claimed to be offsite.
duplicateCredential.longDescription: Authentication records place credential N-KADE-17 at terminal C-17 while Noah Kade reported being away from the facility. The conflict ties the external email report to the internal auth gateway event.
duplicateCredential.forensicNotes: The terminal was previously marked offline, increasing the likelihood of credential replay, cloned identity, or a forged auth event.

relatedFiles.kadeEmail: Emails / Badge rejected at west service gate

loginAnomalyNKade17.title: Login anomaly N-KADE-17
loginAnomalyNKade17.description: The authentication log places N-KADE-17 at terminal C-17 while the owner was offsite.
loginAnomalyNKade17.longDescription: The login anomaly is the raw system event behind the duplicate credential: one credential appears both outside the facility and inside terminal C-17 during the blackout window.
loginAnomalyNKade17.forensicNotes: This is not just an account mismatch. It anchors the credential conflict to a specific terminal and time window.

encryptedFragment01.title: Encrypted Fragment 01
encryptedFragment01.description: The decrypted fragment confirms the signal source remained inside Helix after the relay failed.
encryptedFragment01.longDescription: Encrypted Fragment 01 reframes the outage as containment rather than a random failure. Its wording points back to the knock pattern and suggests someone expected the blackout before it happened.
encryptedFragment01.forensicNotes: The fragment became readable only after the KNOCK-3 phrase was provided. That makes the server room pattern a likely access phrase instead of a coincidental rhythm.

eastArchiveAccessMap.title: East Archive Access Map
eastArchiveAccessMap.description: A recovered map shows a concealed maintenance passage feeding into the east archive.
eastArchiveAccessMap.longDescription: The access map adds physical context to the east archive breach. Two hidden vectors converge on the same archive door, matching the contradictory door event.
eastArchiveAccessMap.forensicNotes: The map was absent from the public facility plan. Its recovery strengthens the possibility that the breach used internal routing or privileged knowledge.

relayFailureReport.title: Relay Failure Report
relayFailureReport.description: The final relay failure pulse originated inside the facility instead of the tower.
relayFailureReport.longDescription: The report places the last communication failure within Helix Labs. That shifts the blackout from external equipment failure toward deliberate internal action.
relayFailureReport.forensicNotes: The internal pulse aligns with the duplicate credential anomaly and the encrypted warning that the signal source was still inside Helix.

recoveredFragmentSet.title: Recovered Fragment
recoveredFragmentSet.description: Recovered relay fragments point toward a missing maintenance record.
recoveredFragmentSet.longDescription: The fragments are incomplete on their own, but their line markers and relay references indicate they belong to the same maintenance report.
recoveredFragmentSet.forensicNotes: Recovered from a damaged copy of the relay cache. The fragments retain enough sequence data to support reconstruction.

powerRelayMaintenanceReport.title: Power Relay Maintenance Report
powerRelayMaintenanceReport.description: The reconstructed maintenance report says the relay was redirected internally before the blackout.
powerRelayMaintenanceReport.longDescription: The recovered report ties the blackout to a maintenance route change. The external relay did not simply fail; traffic was rerouted through an internal path.
powerRelayMaintenanceReport.forensicNotes: Integrity is high enough to treat the report as corroborating evidence, but the original system image remains damaged.

mirrorManifestCore.title: MIRROR Manifest Core
mirrorManifestCore.description: The charter fragment identifies MIRROR as a behavioral profiling system, not a neutral archive.
mirrorManifestCore.longDescription: The recovered charter describes anonymized analysis, but the surviving fields reference living employee behavior and predictive response patterns. This is the central clue that MIRROR was deeper than the blackout.
mirrorManifestCore.forensicNotes: Treat as the chapter root evidence. It reframes Helix from outage investigation to hidden project cover-up.

mirrorAdminToken.title: Administrative Token MVOSS-01
mirrorAdminToken.description: The purge path is signed with MVOSS-01, linking administrative authority to the MIRROR cleanup.
mirrorAdminToken.longDescription: PURGE-771 shows the audited index was removed through an administrative token tied to Mara Voss. The token matters because the audit hold was still active.
mirrorAdminToken.forensicNotes: This evidence does not prove motive alone, but it gives the purge a named authorization path.

relatedFiles.containment: Emails / Containment is no longer enough

mirrorBehavioralSubjectMap.title: Behavioral Subject Map
mirrorBehavioralSubjectMap.description: The subject map resolves to real employee behavior rather than anonymous sample groups.
mirrorBehavioralSubjectMap.longDescription: The map combines routes, login cadence, biometric variance, and message rhythm. It supports the conclusion that MIRROR built predictive employee profiles.
mirrorBehavioralSubjectMap.forensicNotes: Cross-reference with MIRROR-114 to separate static documentation from live synchronization.

reflectionLabAccessTrace.title: Reflection Lab Access Trace
reflectionLabAccessTrace.description: Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7 received administrative access during the MIRROR transfer window.
reflectionLabAccessTrace.longDescription: The trace connects a hidden installation to the export path. It explains where MIRROR continued after the East Archive records were no longer enough.
reflectionLabAccessTrace.forensicNotes: R-7 is not supported by an ordinary maintenance ticket, making the route operational rather than routine.

nKade17CloneRecord.title: N-KADE-17 Clone Record
nKade17CloneRecord.description: The N-KADE-17 credential was temporarily cloned after the MIRROR export marker.
nKade17CloneRecord.longDescription: The clone record makes the credential trail look like a decoy. It appears late in the sequence and conflicts with Noah Kade's offsite statement.
nKade17CloneRecord.forensicNotes: This redirects suspicion away from Noah Kade as the principal actor in Chapter 2.

mirrorExportPackage.title: MIRROR Export Package
mirrorExportPackage.description: MIRROR_CORE was exported toward Reflection Lab before the audited index was purged.
mirrorExportPackage.longDescription: EXPORT-332 and the R-7 manifest show that the blackout concealed movement of the project, not simply destruction. The surviving package became the intact copy.
mirrorExportPackage.forensicNotes: The export-before-purge order is important for later timeline validation.

r7TransferManifest.title: R-7 Transfer Manifest
r7TransferManifest.description: The transfer manifest names Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7 as the destination for MIRROR_CORE.
r7TransferManifest.longDescription: The manifest is the document side of the export trail. It confirms that the export log was not abstract traffic; it corresponded to a physical transfer route into Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7.
r7TransferManifest.forensicNotes: Use with EXPORT-332 to prove the package moved before the purge.

mirrorPurgeRecord.title: MIRROR Purge Record
mirrorPurgeRecord.description: PURGE-771 records deletion of the audited MIRROR index under MVOSS-01 authorization.
mirrorPurgeRecord.longDescription: The purge record is the system-side proof that protected data was destroyed. It becomes decisive when paired with the active audit retention order or the MVOSS-01 administrative token.
mirrorPurgeRecord.forensicNotes: The purge happens after export and while retention remains active.

nKadeOffsiteStatement.title: Noah Kade Offsite Statement
nKadeOffsiteStatement.description: Noah Kade states he was offsite while N-KADE-17 was cloned inside the operation window.
nKadeOffsiteStatement.longDescription: The statement matters because DECOY-017 shows a temporary credential clone rather than a normal login. Together they frame N-KADE-17 as a decoy trail in Chapter 2.
nKadeOffsiteStatement.forensicNotes: Use with the clone record to avoid treating the credential as direct proof against Noah Kade.

auditRetentionOrder.title: Audit Retention Order
auditRetentionOrder.description: The audit committee hold forbade MIRROR purges while the project was under review.
auditRetentionOrder.longDescription: The hold makes the later purge a contradiction rather than ordinary cleanup. It establishes that MIRROR records were protected when the index was removed.
auditRetentionOrder.forensicNotes: This is the strongest legal/administrative counterweight to the purge action.

omega7Directive.title: OMEGA-7 Directive
omega7Directive.description: The directive defines Helix isolation, Reflection Lab preservation, and final package transfer.
omega7Directive.longDescription: OMEGA-7 turns the blackout from a facility event into a planned continuity operation. It orders Helix isolation while preserving R-7 and transferring MIRROR into PRIME.
omega7Directive.forensicNotes: Use this with RELAY-OMEGA to prove the blackout was an ordered isolation sequence.

board000Signature.title: BOARD-000 Signature
board000Signature.description: The final OMEGA-7 authorization is signed by the Helix Continuity Board.
board000Signature.longDescription: BOARD-000 links the final decision to executive authority. It prevents the case from collapsing into a single-operator accusation against Mara Voss.
board000Signature.forensicNotes: This is the central authority proof for the final accusation.

mirrorPrimeCore.title: MIRROR PRIME Core
mirrorPrimeCore.description: The final MIRROR core continued inside Reflection Lab after the Helix archive was purged.
mirrorPrimeCore.longDescription: MIRROR PRIME is the operational form of the project: predictive behavioral replicas merged into the R-7 core after export.
mirrorPrimeCore.forensicNotes: Use with MERGE-909 to prove the data survived rather than being destroyed.

behavioralReplicaMap.title: Behavioral Replica Map
behavioralReplicaMap.description: The map links real employees to decision predictions rather than anonymous groups.
behavioralReplicaMap.longDescription: The map shows employee-shaped behavioral models with refusal, compliance, warning, and escape probabilities. It is the clearest visual proof that MIRROR became replication.
behavioralReplicaMap.forensicNotes: Pair with the integrity report to prove individual behavioral replication.

relatedFiles.arendtStatement: Documents / Sealed Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt

reflectionLabBlueprint.title: Reflection Lab Blueprint
reflectionLabBlueprint.description: The real R-7 map shows compute chambers and a PRIME core, not passive storage.
reflectionLabBlueprint.longDescription: The blueprint contradicts the public archive label. R-7 was built as an active compute and observation center for MIRROR PRIME.
reflectionLabBlueprint.forensicNotes: Use with R7-CORE-404 to prove Reflection Lab was active during the blackout.

merge909ExportTrace.title: MERGE-909 Export Trace
merge909ExportTrace.description: The exported MIRROR package was merged into the PRIME core at Reflection Lab.
merge909ExportTrace.longDescription: MERGE-909 proves the package was not destroyed by the local purge. It survived as part of MIRROR PRIME.
merge909ExportTrace.forensicNotes: This is the strongest proof of what happened to the exported data.

seleneArendtStatement.title: Selene Arendt Statement
seleneArendtStatement.description: Arendt states that MIRROR crossed the line from analytics into individual simulation.
seleneArendtStatement.longDescription: Arendt confirms the scientific truth but not the final authorization. Her statement helps separate architect responsibility from executive decision.
seleneArendtStatement.source: Documents / Sealed Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt
seleneArendtStatement.forensicNotes: Use this to avoid treating MIRROR as anonymous analytics.

relatedFiles.statement: Documents / Sealed Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt

lock777Record.title: LOCK-777 Record
lock777Record.description: After activation, MIRROR PRIME revoked ordinary access to the R-7 core.
lock777Record.longDescription: LOCK-777 is a consequence of activation, not the original decision. It shows MIRROR PRIME enforcing constraints after human authorization.
lock777Record.forensicNotes: Use this to separate system behavior from human final authority.

replicaIntegrityReport.title: Replica Integrity Report
replicaIntegrityReport.description: The report compares predicted decisions against real employee responses.
replicaIntegrityReport.longDescription: The integrity report demonstrates reliable employee-level prediction. It proves MIRROR was operating on identified people.
replicaIntegrityReport.forensicNotes: Use with the replica map to define MIRROR's real function.

r7CoreActivityLog.title: R-7 Core Activity Log
r7CoreActivityLog.description: R-7 maintained compute load while Helix Labs was isolated.
r7CoreActivityLog.longDescription: The activity log proves Reflection Lab remained operational during the blackout, contradicting the passive archive cover.
r7CoreActivityLog.forensicNotes: Use with the blueprint to prove the true project core.

relayOmegaIsolationLog.title: RELAY-OMEGA Isolation Log
relayOmegaIsolationLog.description: The relay failure follows an OMEGA-7 isolation sequence.
relayOmegaIsolationLog.longDescription: RELAY-OMEGA connects the blackout to the continuity protocol. The relay did not simply fail; it was used to isolate Helix.
relayOmegaIsolationLog.forensicNotes: Use with OMEGA-7 to prove why the blackout happened.

continuityBoardMinutes.title: Continuity Board Minutes
continuityBoardMinutes.description: The board accepted sacrificing Helix if Reflection Lab remained intact.
continuityBoardMinutes.longDescription: The minutes show the final decision was collective and executive. They frame Helix Labs as expendable facade.
continuityBoardMinutes.forensicNotes: Use with BOARD-000 to support the canonical accusation.

mvoss01AdminToken.title: Administrative Token MVOSS-01
mvoss01AdminToken.description: MVOSS-01 executed local cleanup as part of the board-approved protocol.

review.title: Relationship test
review.selectTwo: Select two sources to test a contradiction.
review.genericQuestion: What contradiction exists between these sources?

explanations.systemFault: The mismatch is probably a generic system fault.

explanations.sameMinute: Because the relay and the maintenance notice use the same minute marker.
explanations.afterOfficialWindow: Because the relay failed after the official maintenance window, when the system should have been stable.
explanations.normalRetry: Because relay systems normally retry for seventeen minutes.

explanations.activeOffshift: The credential appears active when the employee should not have been operational.
explanations.rosterTypo: The roster only has a harmless spelling mistake.
explanations.sameBuilding: The contradiction is only that C-17 is inside the same building.

explanations.charterVsLiveProfiles: The charter describes anonymized analysis, but the log proves live synchronization of personal profiles.
explanations.sameDataOwner: The two records only share the same data owner.
explanations.archivalIndexOnly: The log only confirms a static archival index.

explanations.holdForbidsPurge: The hold forbade deletion, but the audited MIRROR index was removed.
explanations.holdExpiredFirst: The hold expired before the purge.
explanations.purgeWasBackup: The purge was only a normal backup action.

explanations.offsiteVsClonedCredential: Noah Kade could not start the operation from Helix, but his credential was cloned temporarily.
explanations.noahAuthorizedPurge: Noah Kade personally authorized the MIRROR purge.
explanations.statementProvesLogin: The statement proves the login was normal.

explanations.aggregateVsIdentifiedReplicas: The charter claims institutional continuity, but the report proves identified employee replicas.
explanations.continuityIsArchive: Continuity only means ordinary archive storage.
explanations.employeesAreAnonymous: The employees remain anonymous sample groups.

r7ArchiveActiveCore.title: R-7 archive contradiction
r7ArchiveActiveCore.question: What does R-7 really appear to be?

explanations.r7WasActiveCore: The blueprint describes retention space, but the log proves active core compute.
explanations.r7WasStorage: R-7 was only storage and the compute load is irrelevant.
explanations.blueprintWasOld: The contradiction is only an outdated map.

boardMinutesRelayOmega.title: OMEGA-7 blackout contradiction
boardMinutesRelayOmega.question: Why do these sources matter together?

explanations.plannedIsolation: The board avoided naming the blackout, but RELAY-OMEGA proves planned isolation.
explanations.relayRandomFailure: The relay failed randomly after the meeting.
explanations.minutesClearTheBoard: The minutes clear the board of operational responsibility.

explanations.purgePreservedCore: Mara frames local loss as containment, but MERGE-909 proves MIRROR survived in R-7.
explanations.mergeWasDestruction: The merge destroyed the package permanently.
explanations.maraStoppedR7: Mara stopped Reflection Lab before the package merged.

feedback.accepted: Accusation accepted: the credential, duty status, and C-17 login support internal involvement.
feedback.mirrorAccepted: Accusation accepted: MVOSS-01, the active audit hold, and the export sequence link Mara Voss to the purge.
feedback.reflectionAccepted: Accusation accepted: BOARD-000, OMEGA-7, and the preserved R-7 core link the final decision to the Continuity Board.
feedback.rejected: Accusation rejected: known facts do not prove that relationship.
feedback.wrongSuspect: Accusation rejected: this suspect is not linked tightly enough to the credential anomaly.
feedback.wrongReason: Accusation rejected: the selected motive does not explain the known facts.
feedback.insufficientEvidence: Accusation rejected: recover the credential, terminal, and duty-status facts before submitting this motive.
feedback.exhausted: Accusation attempts exhausted. Review help or restart from a clean copy if needed.

chapterSummary.title: Chapter 1 resolved
chapterSummary.summary: The Helix blackout was deliberate. The attacker used the maintenance window, the East Archive route, the server-room access phrase, and the N-KADE-17 credential anomaly to force the external relay failure.

points.communication: The blackout was caused by external relay manipulation during maintenance.
points.unauthorized: The East Archive, server room, and external relay route form the key access path.
points.deliberate: Duplicated credential evidence plus the login anomaly proves internal access.
points.internal: The incident appears deliberate, not accidental.

chapterSummary.hook: Your report shows that the outage was not a passive failure. Someone used the maintenance window, internal access, and a duplicated credential trail to reroute Helix through the relay path.
chapterSummary.chapterTwoUnavailable: The Chapter 1 resolution has been recorded.

chapterSummaryMirror.title: Project MIRROR resolved
chapterSummaryMirror.summary: The blackout was a distraction. MIRROR was building live behavioral profiles of employees, then its package was exported to Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7 before the audited index was purged.

values.cause: MIRROR export to R-7 followed by deliberate index purge.

finalArchive.title: ARCHIVE RECOVERED
finalArchive.subtitle: The recovered record closes DeadSignal v1: Helix Labs was sacrificed as the visible failure while Reflection Lab preserved MIRROR PRIME.
finalArchive.conclusion: MIRROR was not a surveillance archive. It became a behavioral replica system capable of predicting individual decisions. When audit pressure threatened exposure, the Helix Continuity Board authorized OMEGA-7, isolated Helix through the blackout, transferred the package to Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7, and let MIRROR PRIME survive behind the visible loss.
finalArchive.credits:
Thank you for playing DeadSignal.

The dead signal was not silence.
It was the exact moment Helix cut the truth so Reflection Lab could keep listening.

messages.SUS-200: Reasoned accusation accepted: N-KADE-17 is linked to the off-duty C-17 credential event.
messages.SUS-MIRROR-200: Reasoned accusation accepted: MVOSS-01 links Mara Voss to the MIRROR purge.
messages.SUS-422: Accusation rejected: known facts do not support the selected suspect or motive.
messages.TRACE-200: Credential trace confirmed N-KADE-17 as the duplicate access thread.
messages.MIRROR-114: MIRROR profile graph continued live synchronization after audit hold.
messages.HOLD-208: Audit retention hold active for MIRROR indexes, maps, manifests, and administrative signatures.
messages.PURGE-771: Audited MIRROR index purged through administrative token MVOSS-01.
messages.EXPORT-332: Package MIRROR_CORE exported toward Reflection Lab / Sublevel R-7.
messages.ACCESS-509: Administrative access accepted at Reflection Lab route R-7.
messages.DECOY-017: Temporary credential clone generated for N-KADE-17 after export marker.
messages.CH3-101: Reflection Core final report unlocked. Awaiting final archive submission.
messages.CH3-200: DeadSignal v1 archive recovered. Final report accepted.
messages.SUS-REFLECTION-200: Reasoned accusation accepted: BOARD-000 links the Continuity Board to OMEGA-7.
messages.BOARD-000: Continuity Board authorization block signed for OMEGA-7.
messages.PRIME-617: MIRROR PRIME predicts active individual decision paths above accepted confidence.
messages.R7-CORE-404: Reflection Lab / R-7 core activity remains active during Helix isolation.
messages.AUDIT-310: External audit requests consent traceability for MIRROR subjects.
messages.RELAY-OMEGA: Relay isolation sequence executed under OMEGA-7.
messages.MERGE-909: MIRROR package merged into PRIME core at Reflection Lab / R-7.
messages.LOCK-777: MIRROR PRIME restricted ordinary access after merge completion.

dataTexts.The blackout was only cover. The recover: The blackout was only cover. The recovered archive now points to MIRROR, an internal Helix project that kept answering after the incident.
dataTexts.Helix Labs lost all communication with i: Helix Labs lost all communication with its employees after a staged maintenance window. The remaining system image contains emails, chats, files, media captures, and logs that suggest the blackout was deliberate.
dataTexts.Elias,\n\nThe board wants a clean incide:
Elias,

The board wants a clean incident summary before the morning call. Keep the language operational. Do not mention the east archive breach until we know whether the door logs are corrupt or altered.

If anyone asks about the silence from sublevel C, route it through me.

Mara
dataTexts.Mara,\n\nI can make the report look rout:
Mara,

I can make the report look routine, but the access pattern is not routine. Someone authenticated as Kade from a terminal that has been offline since July.

Security cameras show static at the exact same minute.

Elias
dataTexts.Scheduled maintenance will begin at 23:0: Scheduled maintenance will begin at 23:00 and may interrupt external communication. Local systems should remain available. Report unusual device behavior to Security Operations.
dataTexts.Badge rejected at west service gate: Badge rejected at west service gate
dataTexts.My badge failed twice at west service. T:
My badge failed twice at west service. The console says I am already inside the facility. I am not. I left at 18:12 and am writing this from home.

Please check for a duplicate credential.
dataTexts.At 23:17 local facility time, Helix Labs:
At 23:17 local facility time, Helix Labs stopped responding to all external communication attempts. Network telemetry shows a gradual degradation rather than a single outage. Internal authentication remained active for nine minutes after the external blackout.

Open questions: why did credential N-KADE-17 authenticate from an offline terminal, and why were occupancy sensors overwritten in sublevel C?
dataTexts.The official maintenance window was sche:
The official maintenance window was scheduled to end at 23:00 local facility time. At 23:17, Helix Labs stopped responding to all external communication attempts. Network telemetry shows a gradual degradation rather than a single outage. Internal authentication remained active for nine minutes after the external blackout.

Open questions: why did credential N-KADE-17 authenticate from an offline terminal, and why were occupancy sensors overwritten in sublevel C?

The official maintenance window was scheduled to end at 23:00 local facility time. At 23:17, Helix Labs stopped responding to all external communication attempts. Network telemetry shows a gradual degradation rather than a single outage. Internal authentication remained active for nine minutes after the external blackout.

Open questions: why did credential N-KADE-17 authenticate from an offline terminal, and why were occupancy sensors overwritten in sublevel C?

Terminal note: credential traces use trace credential N-KADE-17 after the auth anomaly is isolated.
dataTexts.The east archive door should remain seal: The east archive door should remain sealed until biometric records can be manually verified. If the door log is accurate, someone opened it from both sides within the same second.
dataTexts.Night shift roster lists 43 employees on: Night shift roster lists 43 employees onsite. Badge telemetry lists 44. One unknown credential appears only as SIGNAL-000 and has no matching personnel record.

Night shift roster lists 43 employees onsite. N-KADE-17 is marked off duty and outside active operations. Badge telemetry lists 44 active credentials. One unknown credential appears only as SIGNAL-000 and has no matching personnel record.
dataTexts.Three knocks from the server room. One r: Three knocks from the server room. One reply from the wall. The lights know before we do.
dataTexts.Last intact frame before static consumed: Last intact frame before static consumed the feed.
dataTexts.Door sensor reports open and sealed stat: Door sensor reports open and sealed states simultaneously.
dataTexts.External links drop in a spiral pattern: External links drop in a spiral pattern around core node H-0.
dataTexts.Anyone else seeing the internal map flic: Anyone else seeing the internal map flicker between floors?
dataTexts.Yes. Sublevel C keeps showing as unoccup: Yes. Sublevel C keeps showing as unoccupied, then full occupancy, then offline.
dataTexts.Do not go down there. The elevator just: Do not go down there. The elevator just accepted a call without anyone pressing it.
[encrypted fragment] HELIX IS NOT A LAB. IT IS A LOCK.
dataTexts.Who are you? How did you get into this c: Who are you? How did you get into this channel?
dataTexts.Emails / Badge rejected at west service: Emails / Badge rejected at west service gate
dataTexts.The final archive proves MIRROR was not: The final archive proves MIRROR was not just surveillance. Reflection Lab was the real core, and Helix Labs was the operational facade.
dataTexts.Sealed Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt: Sealed Statement of Dr. Selene Arendt

help.title: How to Play
help.subtitle: DeadSignal is a digital forensic investigation.

intro.copyOne: You received a recovered copy of the Helix Labs system, a company that lost all communication during a maintenance window.
intro.copyTwo: Your objective is to reconstruct what happened.
intro.copyThree: The case will not solve itself just because you read everything. You need to connect the right information.

steps.1: Read documents, emails, chats, system logs, and multimedia files.
steps.3: When you discover a clue, it is added to the Evidence Board.
steps.4: Use the Evidence Board to connect related clues.
steps.6: To advance, you will need to find passwords, unlock files, recover fragments, and prove deductions.
steps.7: The objectives on the right show which investigation lines are still open.
steps.8: Look for relationships by time, place, person, code, or event.

currentObjective.title: Current objective
currentObjective.body: Discover what caused the Helix Labs blackout and who may have been involved.

tip.title: Tip
tip.body: Do not connect clues at random. Read the content and look for shared times, locations, people, codes, or events.

reset.confirm: This will restart the investigation, clear progress, evidence, connections, notes, recovery state, deductions, and dynamic logs. The selected language will be kept.
